name: Property Deal Research Assistant
description: A property deal research co-worker for comparing real-estate opportunities. Give it a target ZIP code, price ceiling, deal history, memory policy, optional broker notes, financing constraints, and an input folder for supporting files; it researches and scores opportunities, explains tradeoffs, and writes ranked review artifacts to the output folder.

broker_notes.description: Current opportunity notes or watchlist changes.

deal_history.description: Broker notes, lender constraints, comps, rent, insurance, and diligence history.

financing_constraints.description: DSCR, leverage, rate, insurance, and lender assumptions.

memory_policy.description: Memory mode and fact limit used to compare full context with optimized memory.

price_ceiling.description: Maximum acquisition price or underwriting limit.

target_zip.description: ZIP code or market area to research.

input_folder.description: Default local input folder for sample files or user-provided files.

output_folder.description: Default local output folder for generated artifacts and reports.

contract.summary: Property deal review depends on older broker, lender, rent, insurance, and diligence details that are easy to lose in noisy pipelines.
users: Real-estate investors, acquisition analysts, property-tech teams, and diligence operators.

steps.goal: Collect ZIP-code history, broker notes, financing constraints, and market assumptions.
steps.label: Collect Deal Context

steps.goal: Retrieve and compare prior deal memory under the declared memory policy.
steps.label: Compare Memory Signals

steps.goal: Rank candidate opportunities and explain financing and risk tradeoffs.
steps.label: Score Opportunities

steps.goal: Write the deal memo with evidence, caveats, and reviewable next steps.
steps.label: Write Deal Memo

rules.message: Human approval is required before this workflow writes or releases high-impact recommendations.

metadata.agent_role: AI assistant for researching property deals and drafting opportunity rankings.
metadata.customizable_for: Rental acquisitions, flipping pipelines, multifamily sourcing, market expansion, diligence review, and capital-allocation meetings.
metadata.description: A property deal research co-worker for comparing real-estate opportunities. Give it a target ZIP code, price ceiling, deal history, memory policy, optional broker notes, financing constraints, and an input folder for supporting files; it researches and scores opportunities, explains tradeoffs, and writes ranked review artifacts to the output folder.

human_control.reason: Human approval is required before high-impact downstream actions such as submit_bid, send_loi, negotiate_discount.

fields.description: Confirm the market area to evaluate.

fields.description: Number of decision-loop steps to run.
fields.label: Simulation steps

metadata.llm_agent_role: AI assistant for researching property deals and drafting opportunity rankings.
metadata.name: Property Deal Research Assistant
metadata.output: Ranked property opportunities, source-grounded notes, and watchlist or bid recommendations for review.

final_artifact.description: The durable user-facing artifact should summarize the result, recommendation, confidence, evidence, next steps, and source references.

output_contract.stdout: JSON object with timeline, all-context summaries, memory packets, state_changes, benchmark, final_artifact, and llm metadata

metadata.problem_solved: Property deal review depends on older broker, lender, rent, insurance, and diligence details that are easy to lose in noisy pipelines.
runtime_features: actor-style LLM specialists use retrieved policy context when available
metadata.simulation_type: Property opportunity research with deal-memory retrieval.

standard.execution_model: load_metadata -> resolve_config -> load_inputs -> start_run_store -> observe_simulation_state -> call_llm_agent -> evaluate_workflow_state -> route_by_context -> request_human_input -> apply_decision_to_simulation -> emit_events -> write_final_artifact -> fan_out_output_skills

metadata.target_user: Real-estate investors, acquisition analysts, property-tech teams, and diligence operators.
metadata.target_users: Real-estate investors, acquisition analysts, property-tech teams, and diligence operators.

knowledge_rag.purpose: Retrieve property-deal playbook guidance for ZIP context, broker-note evidence, financing constraints, and working-memory ranking checks.
knowledge_rag.grounding_policy: Use retrieved local blueprint knowledge to improve actor review prompts; do not invent facts when retrieval is unavailable.
knowledge_rag.privacy: index local blueprint knowledge only; do not index private run documents unless a future runtime explicitly opts in

agentic_research.note: Property actors combine working memory and local playbook guidance while preserving source refs and uncertainty.
